name: Santander – from La Maruca to the Faro de Cabo Mayor
name_original: Santander – desde La Maruca hasta el Faro de Cabo Mayor
region_intro: Jogging around Costera offers diverse running routes, primarily along the coast and through natural parks. The region features a mix of coastal paths, river trails, and areas with varied elevation, providing different challenges for runners. Landscapes include beaches, marshlands, and park environments. This area is well-suited for outdoor activities, with trails designed for both leisurely jogs and more strenuous runs. Best jogging routes around Costera The most popular jogging route is Santander – running circular along the coast to El Sardinero beach, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete, offering scenic coastal views. Another top favourite among local runners is Santander – circular to Parque Atlántico de Las Llamas, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This route provides a shorter, accessible run through a city park. Local runners also love the Faro del Pescador y Faro del Caballo – running circular through the Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through a natural park with marshlands, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Jogging around Costera is defined by coastal paths, natural parks, and river trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy park loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Costera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 15,000 runners have used komoot to explore Costera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Costera?
A: Costera offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 840 trails available on komoot. These routes cater to all levels, from easy park loops to more challenging runs with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there easy running trails for beginners in Costera?
A: Yes, Costera has over 60 easy running trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999364004/santander-circular-to-parque-atlantico-de-las-llamas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander – circular to Parque Atlántico de Las Llamas&lt;/a&gt;, an accessible 4.2 km (2.6 miles) path through a city park.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on jogging routes in Costera?
A: Jogging in Costera offers diverse landscapes, including scenic coastal paths, tranquil river trails, and routes through natural parks. You'll encounter beaches, marshlands, and varied terrain, providing a rich experience for every runner.

Q: Are there any circular running trails in Costera?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Costera are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999338775/santander-running-circular-along-the-coast-to-el-sardinero-beach" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander – running circular along the coast to El Sardinero beach&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning coastal views on a 10.9 km (6.8 miles) loop.

Q: What are some challenging running routes in Costera?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Costera features over 220 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998993502/faro-del-pescador-y-faro-del-caballo-running-circular-through-the-parque-natural-de-las-marismas-de-santona-victoria-y-joyel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro del Pescador y Faro del Caballo – running circular through the Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 9.3 km (5.8 miles) trail with significant elevation gain through a natural park.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Costera?
A: The running trails in Costera are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 15,000 runners have explored the region's varied terrain, often praising the scenic coastal views and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or landmarks?
A: While specific running routes aren't always designed to pass directly through historical sites, the region of Costera, particularly in Spain, is rich in patrimonial wealth. You can find attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar Historic Town Centre&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/344751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas&lt;/a&gt; nearby, offering opportunities to combine your run with cultural exploration.

Q: Can I find running routes along rivers in Costera?
A: Yes, Costera offers beautiful river trails for jogging. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999033419/running-along-the-nansa-river-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running along the Nansa River river trail&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a serene 9.1 km (5.7 miles) experience alongside the Nansa River.

Q: Are there any coastal running trails with lighthouses in Costera?
A: Yes, you can enjoy coastal runs that feature lighthouses. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999356381/santander-from-la-maruca-to-the-faro-de-cabo-mayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander – from La Maruca to the Faro de Cabo Mayor&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.3 km (7 miles) route that takes you towards the iconic Faro de Cabo Mayor, offering stunning sea views.

Q: What are some popular coastal running trails in Costera?
A: Beyond the popular El Sardinero beach route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999084067/costa-quebrada-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Quebrada Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a fantastic option. This moderate 7.5 km (4.7 miles) trail showcases the rugged beauty of the coastline. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999103830/senda-del-litoral-miengo-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Litoral: Miengo Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.6 km (4.7 miles) moderate route.

Q: Are there running routes that go through natural parks or preserves in Costera?
A: Yes, Costera is home to several natural parks that offer excellent running opportunities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998984589/fuerte-de-san-carlos-y-mirador-pena-ganzo-circular-by-the-parque-natural-de-las-marismas-de-santona-victoria-y-joyel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuerte de San Carlos y mirador Peña Ganzo – circular by the Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 7.7 km (4.8 miles) route through a significant natural marshland park.
tips:
The lighthouse was lit for the first time on August 15, 1839. Currently, it houses different exhibitions about lighthouses that can be visited.

Nearby there is a restaurant that is very popular among Santander residents for midday meals.
A small, beautiful and quiet cove.

Fantastic area where you can walk and enjoy the views at any time of the year, breathe the smell of the sea, eat in one of its restaurants and even take a bath on its beach, which is small and with a special charm since most of it it is on rock, with hardly any sand.
